summary: A well-constructed, test-only golden replay test for the replslots report screen. The synthetic tar harness faithfully mirrors the in-repo bgwriter example, the 15-column layout matches internal/query/replication_slots.go exactly, and the test genuinely proves all four claimed properties (col 6-13 diff, slot_name pairing, retained,KiB DESC ordering, and the physical-slot coalesced-zero case). Both tests pass and the full report package shows no regressions.

suggestions.suggestion: Test function names are Test_app_doReport_ReplSlots / _ReplSlots_empty, while the task file (lines 54, 73, 87-92) specifies the lowercase Test_app_doReport_replslots / _replslots_empty. This is a deviation from the task spec naming. It is harmless (the implementation hint at task line 182 only requires the substring 'ReplSlots' so that -run ReplSlots matches both, which it does), and the existing report_test.go mixes both casings, so there is no real consistency win either way. Worth a one-line note in the decisions log per Post-completion, but not worth changing.
suggestions.benefit: Exact alignment between task file identifiers and code; avoids a reader noticing the casing mismatch.

suggestions.suggestion: The empty-case test (Test_app_doReport_ReplSlots_empty) duplicates the whole tar-building boilerplate (metaRes, writeEntry closure, Config, app setup) from the main test. The bgwriter example avoids this by using a table-driven single function, but the two replslots cases differ enough (row payloads, distinct assertions) that a shared helper would add indirection. Acceptable as-is; if a third replslots replay case is ever added, extract a buildTar(meta, ticks...) helper.
suggestions.benefit: Less copy-paste if the suite grows; lower risk of the two cases drifting apart.

suggestions.suggestion: TsStart/TsEnd use time.Now().Location() to build the window. This matches the bgwriter and procpidstat examples (same pattern), so it is consistent with the codebase. Note only: golden replay output is timezone-independent here (the printed timestamp is derived from the filename, and the window is generous), so this carries no determinism risk for this test.
suggestions.benefit: Documents that the time.Now().Location() use is intentional and risk-free, matching existing harnesses.
